accept: CLAUDE.md's canonical work loop includes an explicit push step (probably step 7: git push origin main, then step 8: repeat).
External-obligation escape condition is reworded to exclude same-branch pushes within this repo.
Rationale for per-issue push (concurrent agents see closes immediately; session boundaries don't lose work) is captured in the versioning-rationale section.
payload.description: Sub-agent on first dogfood run (2026-05-10, act-6bbd) committed and closed cleanly but did not push. Reason: CLAUDE.md's 7-step canonical loop ends at 'act close' + 'repeat from step 2', never mentioning git push origin main. The agent followed the loop exactly. Compounding ambiguity: the 'external obligation' escape condition lists 'publishing a release, pushing a tag' which a reader might generalize to all push operations. Need CLAUDE.md to (a) make per-issue push explicit in the loop, (b) clarify 'external obligation' means cross-repo / public-facing, not same-branch pushes that are part of the established session pattern.
payload.title: CLAUDE.md missing push step in canonical loop
